title: Metallic compounds and metallic matrix composites made using compression activated synthesis
abstract: Articles are manufactured using self-propagating high-temperature synthesis (SHS) reactions. Particulates including reactants can be blended to form a particulate blend. The particulate blend can be preformed. The preform article can be heated to a pre-heat temperature being below an auto-activation temperature and above a minimum compression activated synthesis temperature. Compressive stress can be exerted on the preform article at the pre-heat temperature to initiate the SHS reaction between the reactants and thereby form a product metallic compound. At approximately peak temperature, a flow stress of the product metallic compound can be exceeded to substantially reduce porosity and thereby form a shaped substantially dense article.
claims: Claims CLAIMS We claim: 1 . A method of manufacturing an article, the method comprising: providing a first particulate comprising a first reactant that is a metallic element or metallic chemical compound; providing a second particulate comprising a second reactant that is a second metallic element or metallic chemical compound; blending the first and second particulates to form a particulate blend; preforming the particulate blend to form a preform article; heating the preform article to a pre-heat temperature being below an auto-activation temperature and above a minimum compression activated synthesis temperature; and exerting compressive stress on the preform article at the pre-heat temperature to (i) initiate a self-propagating high-temperature synthesis reaction between the first and second reactants and thereby form a product metallic compound, and (ii) at approximately peak temperature, exceed a flow stress of the product metallic compound to reduce porosity of the product metallic compound and thereby form the article. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein, at the pre-heat temperature, the first and second reactants are extant in solid form. 3. The method of claim 1 , wherein, at the pre-heat temperature, the first reactant is extant in liquid form, and the second reactant is extant in solid form. 4. The method of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein, prior to the step of blending, the first particulate has a mean particle size of between about 1 Î¼Î¹Î· and about 100 Î¼Î¹Î·. 5. The method of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein, prior to the step of blending, the second particulate has a mean particle size of between about 0.1 Î¼Î¹Î· and about 3 Î¼Î¹Î·. 6. The method of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein, prior to the step of blending, the mean particle size of the first particulate is at least three times the mean particle size of the second particulate. 7. The method of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the first particulate has an elastic modulus that is less than an elastic modulus of the second particulate. 8. The method of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the first particulate has a melting temperature that is less than a melting temperature of the second particulate. 9. The method of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the first particulate consists of at least 95% (w/w) of the first metallic element or metallic chemical compound, and the second particulate consists of at least 95% (w/w) of the second metallic element or metallic chemical compound. 10. The method of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the step of preforming comprises at least one of cold pressing and hot pressing the particulate blend to form the preform article. 1 1 . The method of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the step of blending comprises blending an additive agent to the particulate blend. 12. The method of claim 1 1 , wherein the additive agent conveys at least one structural and/or functional material property to the article. 13. The method of claim 1 1 or 12, wherein the additive agent in the self- propagating high-temperature synthesis reaction chemically reacts with at least one of the first reactant and the second reactant. 14. The method of claim 1 1 or 12, wherein the additive agent in the self- propagating high-temperature synthesis reaction does not chemically react with the first reactant and the second reactant. 15. The method of any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein the self-propagating high-temperature synthesis reaction is characterized by a ÎÎ < 0 and a AG < 0. 16. The method of any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein, after the step of preforming, the preform article has a near net shape. 17. The method of any one of claims 1 to 16, wherein the step of exerting comprises maintaining the compressive stress approximately constant for a period starting approximately when the auto-activation temperature is achieved and ending approximately when the article has been formed. 18. The method of any one of claims 1 to 16, wherein the step of exerting comprises increasing the compressive stress during a period starting approximately when the auto-activation temperature is achieved and ending approximately when the article has been formed. 19. The method according to claim 17 or 18, wherein the period lasts from about 1 second to about 1 minute. 20. The method of any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein at least one of the first and second reactants consists substantially of two or more bonded metallic elements. 21 . The method of any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein at least one of the first and second reactants consists substantially of a metallic element bonded to a non-metallic element. 22. The method of any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein at least one of the first and second reactants consists substantially of a metallic element selected from the group consisting of Ag, Al, Fe, Mg, Ni, and Ti. 23. The method of any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein at least one of the first and second reactants consists substantially of a metallic element selected from the group consisting of Ag, Al, Fe, Mg, Ni, and Ti bonded to another metallic element. 24. The method of any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein at least one of the first and second reactants consists substantially of a metallic element selected from the group consisting of Ag, Al, Fe, Mg, Ni, and Ti, the metallic element bonded to a non-metallic element. 25. The method of any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein at least one of the first and second reactants is selected from the group consisting of a metal-boride, a metal-carbide, a metal-nitride, and a metal-oxide. 26. The method of any one of claims 1 to 25, wherein the product metallic compound consists substantially of two chemically bonded metallic elements. 27. The method of any one of claims 1 to 26, wherein the product metallic compound consists substantially of a metallic matrix composite. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the metallic matrix composite comprises a mechanically blended reinforcement. 29. The method of claim 27 or 28, wherein the metallic matrix composite comprises an in situ formed reinforcement. 30. The method of any one of claims 1 to 1 9, wherein the first reactant consists substantially of Al, the second reactant consists substantially of Ti0 2 , and the product metallic compound consists substantially of a metallic matrix composite comprising TiAI in situ reinforced with Al 2 0 3 . 31 . The method according to any one of claims 1 to 19 wherein the article has a porosity of 2% or less. 32. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 19 wherein the article has a porosity of 1 % or less. 33. An article manufactured by the method of any one of claims 1 to 32. 34. The article of claim 33, wherein the article is substantially dense and shaped. 35. The article of claim 34, wherein the article has a porosity of about 2% or less. 36. The article of claim 34, wherein the article has a porosity of about 1 % or less. 37. The article of any one of claims 33 to 36, comprising less than about 1 % (w/w) of unreacted first and second reactants. 38. An apparatus, method and/or composition comprising any combination of one or more of the features described above and/or claimed above and/or illustrated in the drawings.
